title: viola odorata subsp. collina
intro: Viola odorata subsp. collina, also known as the upland violet, is a subspecies of the sweet violet plant that belongs to the violet family (Violaceae). It is a perennial herb with fragrant flowers that have been used for centuries for their medicinal and health-promoting properties. The roots and leaves of the plant contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Viola odorata subsp. collina is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves to support overall health and well-being, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
